bear_case: The bear case here is against our own verdict. We are killing a technically credible file on the strength of two timestamps and a semantic reading of a forum comment. 'Pre-revenue' is a word founders use loosely - it can mean 'no ARR I would defend in a data room', which is entirely compatible with $40K of early contracts they had stopped counting. We never asked. We ran a validator, produced a verdict with the word CONTRADICTED on it, and stopped. If we are wrong, we have not merely missed a company: we have built a machine that reaches confident conclusions about honesty from public text without ever speaking to the person, and we will do that again at scale.
weakest_evidence: The counter-evidence is a forum comment, not a financial record. It is independent of the deck but it is still the founder talking.
'Pre-revenue' and '$40K ARR' are only strictly incompatible under one reading of 'pre-revenue'. We picked that reading without asking.
The 140% NRR claim is marked unverifiable on the basis of our own reasoning rather than any source we found. We should be clear that this is inference.
The founder axis fell from 0.71 to 0.58 on the basis of statements, not on any change in the artifact. The code did not get worse.
load_bearing_claim: That the founder's own May statement refers to the same revenue the March deck asserted. Everything - the verdict, the ranking, the no-call - rests on that single reading, and one email would settle it.
spread_note: The idea-vs-market spread is the widest in the set precisely because the bull and bear readings of one sentence diverge completely.
what_would_change_my_mind: Invoice-level evidence for the March position, or a direct account of what changed.
The founder confirming that 'pre-revenue' meant design partners only and that the deck number was aspirational - which is a different and much smaller problem.
A pattern check: if this is the only instance and the rest of the file is clean, that is a different founder than someone with a habit.
process_objection: This verdict was reached without contacting the company. The memo says so, and that disclosure should stay in front of the partner rather than in a footnote.
